events.explanation: Unusually large magnetic field: B_max = 68.3 nT (4.6σ above mean). Possible causes: magnetospheric compression, flux tube boundary, current sheet crossing, or reconnection inflow region.

59 electron holes (bipolar E∥ events) detected in 6 hours. This is unusually high activity, indicating an active or recently saturated two-stream instability. These phase-space vortices are the direct nonlinear saturation products of Langmuir wave trapping (Bernstein et al. 1957, Graham et al. 2016 GRL).

Fast electron beam: mean V_e = 488.3 km/s > 300 km/s threshold. Ratio v_beam/v_th = 0.1077. When v_beam >> v_thermal, the electron velocity distribution function develops a positive slope df/dv > 0 at v_phase = ω_pe/k, which is the classical two-stream instability condition. Theory (Eq.18) predicts maximum growth rate γ_max at k_res = ω_pe/v_beam.
Unusually large magnetic field: B_max = 41.8 nT (15.7σ above mean). Possible causes: magnetospheric compression, flux tube boundary, current sheet crossing, or reconnection inflow region.

Fast electron beam: mean V_e = 687.5 km/s > 300 km/s threshold. Ratio v_beam/v_th = 0.2179. When v_beam >> v_thermal, the electron velocity distribution function develops a positive slope df/dv > 0 at v_phase = ω_pe/k, which is the classical two-stream instability condition. Theory (Eq.18) predicts maximum growth rate γ_max at k_res = ω_pe/v_beam.
